description:
GOAL: Replace today's Rc<FzMap> linear-scan map with a heap-allocated sorted-array map.

## Decisions captured

- SORTED-ARRAY for v1 (NOT HAMT). Atomic ticket; HAMT can come later as a performance follow-up if profiling shows hot map access.
- Layout: HeapKind::Map; payload = entry_count:u32 + padding + (key:FzValue, val:FzValue) pairs sorted by key (stable order via FzValue total ordering).
- Builtins (already in interp): map_get / map_put / map_has / map_size. Wire to IR prims.
- Persistent: map_put returns a NEW map; existing map untouched. Sorted-array means O(N) put for v1; acceptable for small maps.

## Deliverables

- IR prim or builtin for map ops: Prim::AllocMap, Prim::MapGet, Prim::MapPut, etc.
- Codegen: alloc a new map from N (key, val) pairs sorted; map_put builds a new buffer.
- HeapKind::Map tracer: walks entries, traces both key and val FzValues.

## Tests

- `%{a: 1, b: 2}` allocates and reads.
- map_put returns a new map; original is unchanged (persistent).
- GC traces both keys and values.
- Equality between maps (deep) works correctly.

## Out of scope

- HAMT for large maps (P3 follow-up after performance measurement).
- Map pattern matching syntax (Pattern::Map already exists; reuse via .11.4 lowering).
title: Maps as heap type (sorted-array v1)
